meta.title: About — Safraz Razik
meta.description: AI-accelerated software engineering by Safraz Razik. 15+ years of full-stack development and startup co-founding.

hero.description: Founder of Zetmel, an AI-accelerated software engineering studio. 15+ years of full-stack development, project leadership, and startup co-founding.

intro.heading: Engineering with AI acceleration
paragraphs: I'm Safraz Razik — founder of Zetmel, an AI-accelerated software engineering studio. I'm a software professional with 15+ years in full-stack development, project leadership, and startup co-founding.

My expertise spans modern JavaScript frameworks (React, Vue, TypeScript, Next.js), backend systems (Node.js, PHP, Python), and enterprise platforms like Oracle Commerce Cloud and Oracle Storefront Framework.

What sets my work apart today is the integration of AI into the engineering process. I run local LLM environments (Ollama, OpenCode, Claude Code), evaluate coding models (Qwen, Llama, DeepSeek) for code quality and task fit, and use structured prompting and context-management workflows.

facts.title: Open Source
facts.description: Vue File Agent, Rockplate, and more.

principles.heading: How I work
principles.lead: Three principles guide every project I take on.

items.title: Deep Experience
items.description: 15+ years shipping production software across the stack — web, mobile, desktop, and enterprise. Seasoned engineering judgment to guide every project.

items.title: AI-Accelerated
items.description: Local LLM environments (Ollama, OpenCode, Claude Code) with structured prompting and context-management workflows. Model evaluation for code quality and task fit.

items.title: Production-Ready
items.description: Technical audits, structured milestones, and recovery sprints ensure every deliverable meets production standards. No shortcuts on quality.

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.lead: Common questions about working with me.

items.question: What types of projects do you take on?
items.answer: I build web applications, mobile apps, developer tools, and enterprise solutions. Recent work includes B2B commerce platforms, open-source libraries, and AI-accelerated development workflows.

items.question: How does AI fit into your workflow?
items.answer: I use local LLM environments (Ollama, OpenCode) for research, architecture, implementation, debugging, code review, and technical auditing. Every AI-generated change goes through a structured review before reaching production.

items.question: What's your experience with enterprise platforms?
items.answer: I spent 4+ years at Motorola Solutions as a Senior Frontend Engineer, leading the transition from legacy Oracle Commerce Cloud to a React-based Oracle Storefront Framework.

items.question: What open-source tools have you built?
items.answer: I created Vue File Agent (a Vue.js file upload component) and Rockplate (a JavaScript template engine). Both are available on GitHub.

items.question: Do you contribute to open source?
items.answer: Yes. Beyond my own projects, I contribute to libraries and tools I use in production. Open source is a core part of how I learn and give back to the community.

openSource.heading: Tools I've built
openSource.description: Libraries and components released to the developer community.

cards.title: Vue File Agent

cards.title: Rockplate
cards.text: A lightweight JavaScript template engine for Node.js and the browser, focused on simplicity and performance. View on GitHub

meta.title: Services — AI-Accelerated Software Engineering
meta.description: AI-accelerated engineering, full-stack development, and open-source tool building by Safraz Razik.

hero.description: 15+ years of engineering experience combined with AI-powered workflows. From enterprise platforms to open-source tools.

design.heading: Senior judgment meets AI speed
paragraphs: I combine 15+ years of engineering experience with local LLM workflows for research, architecture, implementation, debugging, code review, and technical auditing. Every AI-generated change goes through a structured review before reaching production.

I run local LLM environments (Ollama, OpenCode, Claude Code), evaluate coding models (Qwen, Llama, DeepSeek) for code quality and task fit, and use structured prompting and context-management workflows for multi-stage development.

included.text: LLM-powered research and design exploration, validated by senior engineering judgment before implementation begins.
Multi-stage Plan → Scaffold → Implement → Audit workflow reduces errors and produces production-quality code.
Every line of AI-generated code passes a structured review: correctness, security, dependencies, and maintainability.
When audits find systemic issues, I run focused recovery sprints to fix patterns at scale and prevent recurrence.
Open-weight models (DeepSeek, Qwen, Llama) evaluated for code quality, instruction-following, and hardware efficiency.
No cloud subscriptions, no API costs, no data leaving your machine. All workflows run on consumer hardware.

development.heading: Code that ships
paragraphs: I build production applications across the full stack: React, Vue, TypeScript, Next.js on the frontend; Node.js, PHP, Laravel on the backend; and enterprise platforms like Oracle Commerce Cloud.
My work spans web, mobile (React Native), and desktop (Electron) — with experience delivering at Fortune 500 scale and lean startup environments alike.

included.text: Modern component architecture with strict typing, tested patterns, and predictable state management.
Progressive frameworks for projects that need flexibility, performance, and great developer experience.
Backend systems built with Express, Laravel, and Symfony — designed for maintainability and scale.
Oracle Commerce Cloud and Oracle Storefront Framework experience from 4+ years at Motorola Solutions.
React Native for cross-platform mobile; Electron for desktop applications with web technologies.
MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, SQLite — schema design, query optimisation, and REST API architecture.

performance.heading: Built in public
paragraphs: I build and release developer tools as open source. Vue File Agent (Vue.js file upload component) and Rockplate (JavaScript template engine) are the most notable, with many smaller contributions along the way.
Open source is a core part of how I learn and give back. Every tool started from a real problem I needed to solve.

included.text: Feature-rich Vue.js file upload component with drag-and-drop, multi-file support, and image previews. Available on GitHub.
Lightweight JavaScript template engine for Node.js and the browser — zero dependencies, under 5KB.
Contributions to libraries and tools I use in production. Open source is part of my daily workflow.

process.heading: A structured approach
process.lead: Four stages, clear milestones, no surprises.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.description: Understand the problem, constraints, and goals. AI-assisted research to explore options quickly.

steps.title: Architecture
steps.description: Design the solution with senior engineering judgment. LLM-assisted for rapid prototyping and exploration.

steps.title: Build & Audit
steps.description: Iterative development with structured prompting and technical audits at every milestone.

steps.title: Deliver & Iterate
steps.description: Production deployment, documentation handover, and ongoing support as needed.

cta.heading: Have a project in mind?
cta.description: Send a short note about what you're building and what you need. I read every message and reply within a working day.
